speaker: V
text: I was thinking that the way we approach hiring is fundamentally broken because we optimize for skills that are easily replaceable by AI within 18 months. We should instead be hiring for 'biological curiosity'—the things an agent can't simulate yet.

speaker: Founder X
text: That's a wild take. So you're saying the JD should basically be a curiosity test?

speaker: V
text: Exactly. If they aren't obsessed with something outside of work, they'll be obsolete soon.
